error_signature: failed to do request: Get "https://docker-images-prod...: no such host
title: DNS lookup failure in WSL for dev container setup
symptom: When setting up the dev container, an error occurs indicating a DNS lookup failure for the specified host. This prevents the container from pulling necessary images.
applies_when: Attempting to set up a dev container
root_cause: The error occurs due to DNS resolution issues within the WSL environment, which can prevent Docker from accessing external resources needed for the dev container setup. This is a common problem when using WSL, especially with network configurations.
fix_steps: Open a PowerShell instance and enter 'ubuntu' to access the WSL shell.
Run 'ping cloudflarestorage.com' to check if DNS resolves correctly.
If DNS fails, consider resetting WSL or adjusting network settings.
agent_instruction: Check DNS resolution in WSL and reset WSL if necessary.
